text: the bot achieved 95% hold rate (19/20 resolutions) with zero trades taken, compared to 85% hold rate with 1 trade in previous batch, indicating increasingly restrictive entry conditions or deteriorating signal quality

the bot's reasoning frequently cited timing constraints (45s, 65s, 144s remaining) as blocking factors even when directional confidence reached 0.52-0.58, indicating the timing filter may be preventing trades that the directional model weakly supports

the bot took zero trades across 20 resolutions despite two BUY signals (cycles 229, 230, 243) that never filled, suggesting order placement occurred at prices the market didn't reach or liquidity constraints prevented execution

three BUY signals all targeted 'down' direction with confidence 0.62-0.72 and occurred during mid-candle price drops (-0.026%, -0.076%, -0.039%), but none resulted in fills, while the actual outcomes showed mixed results (2 down winners, 1 up winner)

the bot generated BUY signals exclusively on 'down' side (cycles 229, 230, 243) with sizes 12-18 and confidence 0.62-0.72, while showing no BUY signals on 'up' side despite 10 up-winning resolutions in the batch

avg PnL improved by $9.61 from previous batch entirely through holding rather than trading, as hold rate increased from 95% to 100%, suggesting the previous batch's single trade was a significant loser
